column36: We were down to the last scheme twist. I’m glad it didn’t show because we did not have time to play a second attempt. Went for feast hoping for a fast game. It kind of worked.
The required heroes held back the flow of the melk.

This game was very difficult as it felt like getting both decks running in both games didn't show up. First game Clea and Vishtani only had one Vistani card. It was all Psi-Lord who did the heavy lifting to be able to focus enough attack to hit the Mastermind.

Game two was some bad luck as we hit scheme twist after scheme twist. Couldn't get any footing and just ran out of time.
I could say a lot, but I'll just leave it at this and a 1 star rating; Psi-Lord sucks and I hate schemes where you just get lucky by having a twist at the bottom of the deck.

The scenario was stressful, but fun. We ended up having a ton of recruit and it was hard to attack. We did have the ancient one and sent someone to the Astral plane, Which allowed us to attack with recruit. We also got the good card so we could hit the mastermind with only recruit which helped a ton. We ended up getting Galactus also and destroyed one city space and got a bunch of extra attack. This is a really cool mechanism and the evil part of me wants to do it more. Lol.

During both games, the illuminati got stuck on their open warfare side for well over half the game. Between clea, brainstorm and Jessica jones, there was too much draw in the decks and they just couldn't function with the draw penalty.

Both games the city shrunk to two within ten turns, so it was annoying until the losecon happened.  I had a silly idea to lean fully into Switcheroo to find Psi-Lord cards to help negate Strikes and Joe Fixit to fight with recruit but it didn't come together in either game.
Lost first attempt to endless stream of ghost racers, second game managed to stabilize by covering zombies with Walking Wifi and Dark influence of hand, but that took way too long

Lots of zombies to deal with! I had some great games here: I chose the first one mainly because of no escapes. Crystal seemed like the perfect fit here, and she delivered! I essentially had 4 Hero Classes at all times thanks to her Uncommon, and she took over the game alongside Medusa for lots of Throne Favor plays. My second pick, Black Bolt, provided great Attack, too. Second game had some escapes, but I had a cool build with White Tiger, and I did some great plays with the Zombie Nova cards in the Villain Deck, so there's that! Fun games all-round.~

This was a rough setup. It took some significant use of White Tiger's Dark Influence of the Hand to keep Rise villains buried. We got 2 early escapes due to a couple of Rise, but gained control after that. We had a Black Panther/Dr. Strange multicolor deck on one side and a Proxima Midnight/Medusa deck on the other. White Tiger was split.

You don't  need Ghost Racers with this scheme. Lost super quickly on the 1st attempt. 2nd attempt I brought in Yelena and Old Man Logan to hopefully prevent some escapes. It didn't matter. The only reason I won is there were 3 scheme twists in a row near the top of the deck, pure luck. I forgot all 4 twists were done and should've focused on mm hits but I was still worried about escapes.

We really enjoyed this challenge, it was so much fun, we really had to work to keep the rise of the living dead under control. Our 2 escapes didn't happen till near the end of the game, which we were disappointed with, but the second game we had escapes very early on. Our second game we still won but there was only 1 villain card left in the deck so it was by the skin of our teeth.

Yelena Belova and Gwenpool both have ways of covering up my RotLD villains, giving me some semblance of control over the city. I still expected to get absolutely slaughtered, but my decks came together in the late game, especially after some tactical and deliberate respawning of Zombie Madame Hydra to get me some extra KOs. In game 2 I was finished off in fewer than 15 turns, but I managed to squeeze something out in Game one and I'm honestly not sure if winning in either round is something everybody in this pod will be able to do. Great setup, really challenging, lots of fun!

Both of my games were victories, but neither went great. The other victory ended in fewer turns, but had 5 escapes and less PPT. 
One thing I enjoyed about this setup was finally understanding White Tiger's Amulets of the Tiger Gods card! I used to avoid it at all costs, but now I see the benefit of quickly building up your Dark Memories, particularly if you pair it with multi-color Heroes or divided cards.

Brutal Masochist set up! We brought in Black Panther for the dual aspect action to help trigger other effects and help size change the MM. Red Guardian was brought in to help bolster covert cards to help towards MM size change and avoid getting penalized to heavily by the Henchmen. The first game was long and we barely survived (twists were buried deep in villain deck thankfully). However, second game was over by turn 15. Such...a...brutal...set-up!

Rise of the Living Dead is my least favorite Keyword which makes this one of my least favorite Schemes of the game. The lack of reliable ways to mitigate the Scheme in this setup, along with the difficulty of the MM made it a daunting challenge that I don't believe it workable without serious Villain and HQ luck. The first try was an avalanche of bad. The second, we had stabilized, with White Tiger/Gwenpool granting Bystanders and Yelena Belova going Undercover, and had hit the MM once but we were too far behind the Scheme at that point and an ill-timed ST wiped us out. The Scheme would be more manageable if there were more STs and they only took 1 per Twist. As it is, you can easily lose on the first or second ST.

I wanted to lean into Undercover to max my score. First game was in shambles. I questioned everything. 2nd game I was able to pull rare Mockingbird and Blade early thanks to easy recruit from Mockingbird and Quake. Mockingbird thinned and cancelled 2 ST and 1MS. Built up some nice undercover points to help. Hellcat really helped against Firestar.
We both constructed decks with lots of draws/discards, and used Blade to control which villain was to capture baby Hope. We like adapting MM and this setup was fun!

column36: Rough go for me, I blame 100% of it on that one Spider-Man who just wouldn't die!

Pretty fun set up though the MM frequently ending up on Cap's Shield was super annoying. Sabretooth felt like a most pick with ol'Buckethead dragging us down and he really proved his worth!

Game 2 I was able to loop my deck via Angel and Brotherhood discard synergies, shame Bill couldn't be here

Could not do anything to avoid that early escape in either game, (7 attack is a lot within the first 10 turns and both twists were only a couple turns apart), but after the first 2 twists I managed to keep the city empty whenever a new twist came (thank you Hellcat for the warnings). P1 was a Dodge deck with Magneto and Electro that did manage to go infinite on the final turn BUT didn't need it because there was only one MM left and I had enough attack before cycling. P2 was a Blade, Hellcat, and Mocking Bird deck. That was probably the most effective I've used Blade (lot of recruit from fighting in the sewers), but I really wish more Mockingbird cards came up to take advantage of the Hydra villains I was fighting. Overall fun set-up that would have been made even more fun with a better shuffle.

column36: Tough game with lots of luck at play. Had back to back twists that led to escape and got the 14 attack adapting mastermind early. Still fun and Iceman is probably too good. Also reminded how bad Magneto is. Woof!

Magneto is actually way better than others give him credit for. For every one of his cards I bought I either dodged it and drew a new card, or played it to draw a card and get three attack. he didn't take up any space in my deck or the HQ, and was useful. Having all three of these traits in a single hero set is actually pretty rare. I also liked the unintentional theming I made of Sabertooth, Magneto, and X-Force Wolverine teaming together for the greater good of Hope Summers and saving the day from the Hydra Super-Adaptoid

Multiple wounds and lack of KO power resulted in too long of game.  Not our best effort but good fun nonetheless.

Emma Frost is definitely frustrating wasting our good turns away but overall the setup was a good time.

column36: Both games went well, but 1st was particularly good. Didn’t see much of Domino, but Noir Heroes and Rick Jones ended up working really well together, eg they had one great Transform turn that hit both sides of MODOK. Dracula gave me Luke’s rare too and by the end I was deliberately not Outwitting Leader to play more cards from the Villain deck to hit.

Yikes that ended up being tougher than I thought it would! Game 1 went pretty well, as I got the chance to recruit the rares for Rick Jones, Nightcrawler, and Iron Fist! Lots of costs helped shuffle the twists in pretty consistently which was nice. The second game was a big loss though with 3 scheme twists in a row in the mid-game! column36: I thought the interaction between Outwit and Heist was fun once I started getting a decent variety in card values, but before that it was a little overwhelming. It was tricky because MODOK was on his setup side for most of the first half of the game, making outwit even harder. Having both cioies of The Leader show up very early didnt help either. But I did enjoy the setup

MODOK increasing the outwit threshold is brutal early game.  You almost can't wait for a master strike so that he transforms.  This is a surprisingly challenging scheme.  You'd think the straightforward nature of play an extra card from the villain deck is easy enough, but when The Leader pops up and you can't outwit him and you get a string of scheme twists triggering, it's quickly overwhelming, so you make the devil's bargain of shuffling the schemes back in and dealing with them later.  This killed me in the second attempt.  Luckily my first game I had some early transformations for Rick Jones and had a enough power to hit MODOK with some attack, and on the final turn I was able to give him a double hit of knocking out his last tactic with recruit on his transformed side, and immediatley hitting his untransformed side for the final blow.  

Ultimately, too straightforward to be fun based on keywords or a creative scheme, and too frustrating to want to play this combo again any time soon.
I hate MODOK

This setup was deceptively difficult! Iron Fist was a great pick to round out the team. Both players could outwit and heist almost every time they needed to by round 10 or so. We just did not have enough KO power to streamline the decks at all. Miek would have been a good pick, but I already used him in a previous week. I felt really limited this week (more than normal) with my hero selection. I went with Wolverine to get a little bit of KO power and to play well with all the other instinct cards. Not great, but not bad.
Overall, it just took way too long to get going. We just barely won at the end where player 2 managed to get 10 recruit and 10 attack, to hit MODOK twice in the same turn. Both decks just got way too bloated. A bunch of villains escaped early on, but that just happens sometimes. We tried a second attempt, but that just went even worse with some bad luck in the villain deck early on. Lost the game turn 25 when I drew three scheme twists in a row.
Giving this setup a 3. Rick Jones/Luke Cage was a fun combo I've never used before. Would have been more fun maybe by swapping out Underworld and Rings for more rewarding villains (that give better benefits for fighting them). I also really like Nightcrawler...but I had no idea what to do with him in this setup. A few tweaks I think would make this a great setup.

Moving to an imaginary board is hard to manage. First game kicked my butt with an expensive HQ locking it for a while, but then I found my saving grace: Ronan the Accuser. I fought Malekith like three times in the same turn because every time I fought him, my "Rally the Kree Warriors" would give me 4 more shards to work with on top of the large amount of attack I had already made that turn.

This was a very engaging setup, lots of fun!  Despite a string of early Scheme Twists, player 1 was able to snag a couple of Danger Sense cards to help at least somewhat mitigate the Villain deck - more to avoid surprise than anything else.  Lady Sif also was really helpful to build extra attack with all the artifacts flying around.  Player 2 mostly leveraged General Okoye with some Moondragon mixed in, to play a good support role.

Game two had lots of early henchmen which was nice, and I kept both cities very clean. Between that and the schemes moving which city existed, every single villainous weapon save 1 was KOd. Malekith never managed to acquire one, making an easy beatdown. It honestly was pretty underwhelming, making me give just a rank of 3 here from this second game, the scheme while fun, did just make the villainous weapons not work game 2 essentially. column36: Definitely more fun than going against Exodus. One hand was just strictly 4 cost cards and the other was ramping up for one big hand! Both hands were evenly balanced and only one villain escape in this scheme is a big win in my view.

After contemplating the setup and even for the first 10 turns I would have said the fun rating was a 4 or 5. It was a clever combination and I liked the puzzle aspect. However after countless wasted turns in both games it was a total slog and indeed "we never, ever want to play this setup again." Many turns with 14+ strength and nothing to fight (that was going to stay dead at least) and no point in recruiting. Thank goodness for Sue's focus to rescue bystanders to at least farm for points. 

First game my only intent was leaning into the 4s and Sue's card that required you to play a 4... so which heroes had the most 4 cards were how we ended up (kind of randomly) with Wolverine and Strong Guy. After such a slog I decided wait I need to study this more and realized the much more obvious choices of Psi-Lord and Mr. Fantastic. Unfortunately our game with them went even longer at 50 turns and another escape! There is just a bit too much luck in this and it was frustrating realizing someone is going to turn in a 25 turn win with no escapes and nodding my head saying "Yes, that is possible with this" but it just has to break exactly right to pull that off.

I think for this combination to work you need to have a bit more keyword help either from Danger Sense to manipulate the resurrections or Piercing Energy to have more of a backdoor chance at Indestructible Man. Thought about Forge's BFG for that purpose but just couldn't justify 1 card out of 70 that we may never see.

To preface, I was glad to try this setup. It's a really funny idea, and, while I will complain, I really appreciate all the unique setups and the tournament, so thank you to Kylah and Bagel. My complaint may be different than expected. I don't mind getting rolled, that's a fun challenge. My first game went poorly, and was more fun to me than my second. My issue with this scheme and mastermind is how much if a slog it makes it when you've pretty much guaranteed victory. I got really lucky, a few early twists to calm things down and let me get rolling, and my plan worked perfectly. Got enough recruiters and DDs rare came up pretty early, got to mill my starter deck, had a deck where that rare was the only non 4 cost. Very funny. Then I waited like 30 turns for enough Assassins to pop out, and not resurrect. I really enjoyed the planning and the idea, it just stinks when you've clearly won but can't even use the high attack you generate. And just have to wait for so long.

So much to say, so little time because finishing at the wire. Maddening but so creative. Satisfying synergies. Every day I'm shuffling for fateful resurrection and clone. Psi-Lord, Invisible Woman, Human Torch, amazing engine. Shuffling 2 villains back in before checking fateful resurrection on Mastermind is diabolical. 53 turns. 6+ hours to complete. Worth? haha Crazy setup. :D
Fateful Rez on everything was crazy. We're very curious to see how everyone did.

The setup was a good challenge, but not impossible. We had to change our gameplan from game 1 to game 2 to account for what the Mastermind and Scheme were doing, and we were able to find success with Fantomex and Viv Vision in our 2nd match. My partner did most of the work with his Weapon X sequencing, getting 3 or 4 cards in sequence each time. We really used a brains over brawn strategy to make sure Sabretooth and the other Villains didn't get any Berserk bonuses. Also, we got a Skids early and took advantage of the Wounds in this scheme =)
This one was fun, got to mess around with berserk which is something I don't do often.
